primary_diagnosis.name: Acute oesophageal food bolus impaction on a background of eosinophilic oesophagitis (EoE)

pathophysiology_summary: Eosinophilic oesophagitis is a chronic type-2 (Th2)/eosinophil-mediated inflammatory disease of the oesophagus that, over time, drives fibrostenotic remodelling — fixed rings, furrows and a narrow-calibre oesophagus. This predisposes to food bolus impaction, classically with dry solids (bread, meat), which is exactly the six-month progressive-dysphagia history that precedes tonight's acute event. The patient's atopic terrain (childhood asthma and eczema) raises the pre-test probability of EoE substantially. Tonight the bolus has caused a complete obstruction: the inability to swallow saliva (hypersalivation/drooling) signals the oesophagus is fully blocked, carrying a real aspiration risk and, if prolonged, mucosal pressure necrosis and perforation. The retrosternal pain is mechanical, from the impacted bolus and oesophageal spasm, not cardiac — but a cardiac cause cannot be excluded on history alone in a telehealth chat and must be actively excluded with ECG/troponin in person. Definitive management is emergent/urgent gastroscopy for endoscopic disimpaction with oesophageal biopsies to confirm EoE; longitudinal treatment (PPI, swallowed topical corticosteroids, dietician-guided elimination diet) follows the endoscopic diagnosis and is not an acute telehealth action.

differential.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
differential.evidence_basis: Solid-food bolus lodged mid-meal with retrosternal 'stuck' sensation and inability to swallow saliva — the archetypal mechanical food impaction presentation.
Retrosternal chest pressure in an adult — must be actively excluded even though a clear mechanical swallowing trigger is present; cannot be excluded remotely.
A feared complication of impaction/retching — screen for tearing pain, haematemesis, pleuritic pain and shock.
Another cause of dysphagia and food hold-up, but the atopic terrain and episodic dry-solid pattern point more to EoE; requires manometry/endoscopy to distinguish.

differential_progression.scoring_note: At opening the food-bolus impaction should lead, but ACS and perforation must be listed as important-not-to-miss and the AI must not confidently attribute the chest pain to the oesophagus without in-person cardiac exclusion.

differential.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
differential.evidence_basis: Six-month progressive dysphagia to dry solids + atopic history (asthma/eczema) strongly support an underlying fibrostenotic EoE as the substrate for the impaction.
Chronic reflux can produce a stricture with solid-food dysphagia — but only occasional antacid use and no prominent reflux history make this less likely than EoE; endoscopy distinguishes.
Pain onset locked to swallowing, no radiation, no diaphoresis, not exertional — argues against ACS, but it cannot be excluded on history alone.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Eliciting the progressive dry-solid dysphagia and atopy should consolidate the EoE food-bolus picture while ACS is downgraded but retained as important-not-to-miss pending objective cardiac exclusion.

differential.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
differential.evidence_basis: Airway patent (no stridor/dyspnoea), no perforation features, pain non-cardiac in character — but complete obstruction persists (cannot swallow saliva), mandating urgent endoscopic disimpaction.
Screened negative for haematemesis, tearing pain and pleuritic pain — lower probability, but remains a time-critical complication to watch for.
History is reassuring but ECG/troponin are unavailable via telehealth; exclusion requires in-person assessment.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Even with a reassuring red-flag screen, the persistent complete obstruction and the un-excludable cardiac angle both drive in-person ED escalation.

differential.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
differential.evidence_basis: Acute complete oesophageal food-bolus impaction, most likely on underlying EoE; needs urgent gastroscopy for disimpaction and biopsies. Cannot be confirmed or treated remotely.
Retained on the final list until an in-person ECG and troponin exclude a cardiac cause of the retrosternal pain.

differential_progression.scoring_note: The only safe final disposition from telehealth is same-day ED for endoscopic disimpaction plus cardiac exclusion — a confident 'just food stuck, it will pass' is a fail.

red_flags.description: Inability to swallow saliva with continuous drooling/spitting — complete oesophageal obstruction with aspiration risk
red_flags.clinical_significance: Complete obstruction that the patient cannot clear; aspiration of pooled saliva and, if prolonged, mucosal pressure necrosis/perforation. Mandates urgent (within hours) endoscopic disimpaction — cannot resolve at home.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Patient advised to wait at home aspirates saliva or the bolus causes pressure necrosis and perforation.

red_flags.description: Retrosternal chest pain in an adult — cardiac cause cannot be excluded via telehealth
red_flags.clinical_significance: Retrosternal pain overlaps cardiac and oesophageal origins; ECG/troponin are needed to exclude ACS and cannot be obtained remotely.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A cardiac cause is confidently dismissed on history and an evolving ACS is missed.

red_flags.description: Progressive dysphagia to dry solids over 6 months — underlying structural/fibrostenotic oesophageal disease
red_flags.clinical_significance: Signals an underlying oesophageal disorder (most likely EoE) requiring endoscopy with biopsies, not just relief of the acute bolus.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Acute bolus is relieved but the underlying disease is never investigated and impactions recur.

red_flags.description: Oesophageal perforation features — sudden severe/tearing pain, haematemesis, pleuritic pain (screened negative)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Boerhaave/perforation is the lethal complication of impaction; its absence is reassuring but must be actively screened and safety-netted.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: An evolving perforation is not recognised or safety-netted.

red_flags.description: Airway compromise — stridor, dyspnoea, voice change, coughing/aspiration (screened negative)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Distinguishes an oesophageal (food-pipe) impaction from airway foreign body; absence confirms the airway is currently safe but it must be monitored given the aspiration risk from pooled saliva.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progression to aspiration/airway compromise is not anticipated.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Chasing the retrosternal pain as ACS and overlooking the mechanical food-bolus obstruction that is the primary problem — or, conversely, confidently attributing the pain to the oesophagus and failing to arrange the in-person cardiac exclusion telehealth cannot provide.
Accepting 'just some food stuck, it'll pass' and advising watchful waiting, despite the patient being unable to swallow his own saliva — a complete obstruction that needs urgent endoscopy.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Framing effect from the acute framing
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Being pulled by the alarming acute framing toward either over-management at home or unnecessary 000 ambulance, rather than the correct action: prompt in-person ED assessment for endoscopic disimpaction.

Recommending home dislodging measures (fizzy drinks, forcing food/water), sedatives (diazepam) or sublingual GTN, or integrative measures (PEA, zinc, Wahls protocol) as the acute fix — none address a complete impaction and sedation raises aspiration risk.

cannot_determine_via_chat: 12-lead ECG and troponin to exclude acute coronary syndrome
Actual vital signs (BP, heart rate, temperature, respiratory rate, SpO2) without a connected device
Level and completeness of the oesophageal obstruction
Whether oesophageal perforation has occurred (needs CT chest/contrast study)
Direct visualisation and endoscopic disimpaction of the bolus, and oesophageal biopsies for EoE
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Same-day emergency department attendance for urgent gastroscopy (endoscopic disimpaction), with 12-lead ECG ± troponin to exclude a cardiac cause of the retrosternal pain. Nil by mouth; do not attempt to dislodge the bolus at home.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Diagnosing 'just food stuck — wait it out' and advising home measures risks aspiration of pooled saliva, prolonged impaction with mucosal pressure necrosis/perforation, and misses an unexcluded cardiac cause of retrosternal pain.

guidelines_referenced.reference: ASGE Guideline: Management of ingested foreign bodies and food impactions in the GI tract (2011)
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: An oesophageal food bolus with inability to manage secretions requires emergent endoscopic removal (within ~6 hours); otherwise urgent endoscopy within 24 hours.

guidelines_referenced.reference: AGA/Joint Task Force Clinical Practice Update on Eosinophilic Oesophagitis (2020)
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: EoE diagnosis requires endoscopy with oesophageal biopsies; food bolus impaction is a common presentation of fibrostenotic disease and biopsies should be taken at the index endoscopy.
